Sandy Springs Spring Cleaning Guide 2025

Your complete spring cleaning guide for Sandy Springs homes. Room-by-room checklist to refresh your home for the season.

When spring arrives in Sandy Springs, it brings blooming dogwoods, warmer days, and an unmistakable layer of yellow-green pollen on every surface. For homeowners across neighborhoods like Riverside, Powers Ferry, and River Springs, this is the signal to open the windows, reset the house, and tackle the seasonal deep clean your home has been waiting for all winter. This guide walks you through a practical, room-by-room approach to spring cleaning that addresses the specific challenges of living in the greater Atlanta area.

Start With a Pollen Strategy

Sandy Springs is consistently ranked among Georgia's highest pollen count areas, thanks to its dense tree canopy of pine, oak, and birch. Before you begin cleaning indoors, take a few proactive steps to reduce pollen infiltration throughout the season.

  • Replace HVAC filters with MERV 11 or higher rated filters before turning on the AC for the first time.
  • Seal window gaps and check weatherstripping on exterior doors. Even small gaps invite pollen inside.
  • Wash exterior entry mats and consider placing a second mat inside each doorway to trap particles.
  • Schedule an air duct cleaning if it has been more than two years since your last service.

Addressing pollen at the source will make every room in your house easier to maintain for the rest of the season. If you struggle with allergies, a professional deep cleaning service can eliminate embedded allergens that regular vacuuming misses.

Kitchen: The Heart of the Reset

Winter cooking tends to leave behind grease buildup, cluttered pantries, and neglected appliance interiors. Spring is the perfect time to restore your kitchen to a fresh baseline.

  • Pull out the refrigerator and clean behind and underneath it, clearing dust from coils
  • Empty the pantry, wipe shelves, and discard expired items
  • Deep clean the oven interior using a baking soda and vinegar paste
  • Degrease range hood filters by soaking them in hot soapy water
  • Sanitize the dishwasher by running an empty cycle with white vinegar
  • Scrub grout lines on tile backsplashes and floors with a stiff brush

Bathrooms: Moisture and Mildew Control

Georgia's humidity makes bathrooms particularly vulnerable to mold and mildew growth, especially during the transition from winter to spring. Focus on ventilation and surfaces that trap moisture.

  • Inspect caulking around tubs, showers, and sinks. Re-caulk any areas showing discoloration or gaps.
  • Clean exhaust fans by removing the cover and vacuuming dust buildup from the blades.
  • Scrub tile grout with hydrogen peroxide or a commercial mildew remover.
  • Wash shower curtains and liners in the washing machine on a gentle cycle with baking soda.
  • Organize medicine cabinets and discard expired medications safely.

Bedrooms and Living Areas

After months of closed windows and recirculated air, bedrooms and living spaces accumulate dust in places you rarely notice. A thorough spring cleaning should address both visible surfaces and hidden areas.

  • Flip or rotate mattresses and vacuum both sides
  • Wash all bedding, including pillows, mattress pads, and duvet inserts
  • Dust ceiling fans, light fixtures, and the tops of door frames
  • Move furniture away from walls to vacuum and mop behind it
  • Clean window blinds slat by slat using a damp microfiber cloth
  • Wipe down baseboards and crown molding throughout the house

For homes with hardwood floors common in Sandy Springs neighborhoods like Dunwoody Panhandle, use a pH-neutral wood cleaner rather than vinegar-based solutions, which can dull the finish over time.

Outdoor Spaces and Entryways

Your front porch, back deck, and garage are extensions of your living space and deserve attention during spring cleaning. Sandy Springs homes near the Chattahoochee River corridor often deal with extra moisture and organic debris.

  • Pressure wash driveways, walkways, and patios to remove winter grime and pollen buildup.
  • Clean exterior windows inside and out for maximum natural light.
  • Sweep and organize the garage, disposing of items that have accumulated over winter.
  • Wipe down front door hardware, house numbers, and porch light fixtures.

When to Call in the Professionals

A DIY spring clean works well for maintenance-level tasks, but some jobs benefit from professional equipment and expertise. Consider scheduling a regular cleaning service to maintain the results of your spring reset throughout the season. For homes that have not had a thorough cleaning in several months, a professional deep cleaning will tackle the heavy lifting so you can focus on organizing and refreshing your space.
